payload.summary: Accept fr-19-b035a585 and fr-19-4993d634 because they expose real attempt-identity collisions that can reject or overwrite distinct runs. Reject fr-19-b353e532 because removing all started_at-based legacy correlation would break required idempotent repair for runless attempts without introducing any replacement identifier.

votes.rationale: `matching_finalized_task_runs` currently falls back from an exact `run_id` lookup to any terminal row with the same `started_at`. That is too broad when the caller supplied a concrete `run_id`: a finalized row for `run-2` can be treated as if it were the caller's `run-3` attempt purely because the timestamps match. Elsewhere the code treats distinct named `run_id`s as distinct attempts, so this fallback can falsely block a legitimate new run. Restricting the fallback to runless terminal rows preserves the intended legacy backfill case without conflating different named attempts.

The collision concern is real, but this amendment removes the only correlation key the system has for legacy runless attempts. The current `started_at` path is what allows idempotent repair of partial start writes, replay of partial completion writes, and pairing of a legacy running row with its later terminal row. Eliminating that logic wholesale would turn same-attempt replays into duplicate attempts and regress existing recovery behavior. A sound fix would need a new durable attempt identifier for runless entries first; removing `started_at` matching by itself is not safe.

`append_event_if_missing` and `repairable_completion_event` do not key completion repair on a real attempt identity. They effectively use `timestamp + event_type + bead_id`, then merge details as long as project/outcome are compatible. That means two distinct attempts for the same bead can be collapsed if they finish at the same timestamp and one event is missing `run_id`, causing the second completion to be dropped or overwritten in the journal. Since the journal is part of the audit trail, dedupe/repair must be tied to an actual attempt identifier or disabled for this case.
